V-M-ASSEMBLE.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure code generation + sandbox execution, fully deterministic given valid inputs

scenarios.scenario-1: LLMClient.call(prompt="hello") with a stubbed httpx transport returning {choices:[{message:{content:"world"}}]} produces LLMResponse(text="world", model=..., usage=..., duration_ms>=0)
scenarios.scenario-2: httpx transport returning HTTP 500 raises LLMCallError with status_code=500 and the error body in the message
scenarios.scenario-3: httpx transport raising httpx.TimeoutException raises LLM_TIMEOUT (or LLMCallError carrying timeout context)
scenarios.scenario-4: LLMClient never logs api_key or full prompt at INFO; redaction policy applies (api_key never logged at any level; prompt and response text only at DEBUG)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never log api_key, full prompt, or full response at INFO or above
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never silently swallow non-2xx responses

V-M-LLM.autonomy-assessment: NOT YET SWARM-SAFE as a verified module — source is bounded and stub-friendly, but tests/unit/test_llm.py is absent; add the planned deterministic httpx transport tests before assigning LLM changes to autonomous workers

scenarios.scenario-1: mint validate fixture.docx --severity=lenient prints ValidationReport JSON to stdout, exit 0 on pass / non-zero on fail
scenarios.scenario-2: mint fingerprint fixture.docx prints FingerprintResult JSON, exit 0
scenarios.scenario-3: mint create docx "prompt" --tier=frontier --model-response-file fixture.js prints CreateResult JSON; output file exists at the path inside the JSON
scenarios.scenario-4: mint extract fixture.docx prints DesignTokens JSON
scenarios.scenario-5: mint create with unknown subcommand or missing required arg exits CLI_USAGE_ERROR (non-zero) and prints argparse-style usage to stderr
scenarios.scenario-6: mint validate /nonexistent/path.docx exits CLI_FILE_NOT_FOUND (non-zero) with a clear error line on stderr
scenarios.scenario-7: (Phase-5) mint edit fixture.docx --plan plan.json prints EditResult JSON; output_path inside the JSON points to an existing file
scenarios.scenario-8: (Phase-6) mint validate fixture.docx (no --engine, no MINT_ENGINE) exits non-zero; stderr contains "MINT_ENGINE=python" and "Phase 0"; no output file created
scenarios.scenario-9: (Phase-6) mint --engine python validate fixture.docx — same as scenario-8
scenarios.scenario-10: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE=js mint validate fixture.docx — succeeds (env supplies engine)
scenarios.scenario-11: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE=python mint --engine js validate fixture.docx — succeeds (CLI overrides env via os.environ pre-load)
scenarios.scenario-12: (Phase-6) mint --help shows top-level `--engine {python,js}` flag with help text mentioning MINT_ENGINE
scenarios.scenario-13: (Phase-6) NEGATIVE TRACE: mint validate fixture.docx (no --engine, no MINT_ENGINE) — captured stderr contains [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] and NotImplementedError but does NOT contain any [Validate], [Create], [Sandbox], [OOXML], [Edit], or BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE / BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE / BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK markers. Proves the chokepoint runs BEFORE any cmd_* would have started side effects.
scenarios.scenario-14: (Phase-6) ARG POSITION: mint validate --engine js fixture.docx (--engine after subcommand) → CLI_USAGE_ERROR (non-zero), argparse usage to stderr. --engine is top-level only.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never crash with an unhandled exception leaking traceback to stdout (stderr is acceptable for genuine bugs)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never print non-JSON content to stdout for the JSON-emitting subcommands

V-M-CLI.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin dispatcher; integration tests cover end-to-end behavior

scenarios.scenario-1: unpack(minimal_valid_docx) → returns parts list including [Content_Types].xml, word/document.xml, word/styles.xml; all XML files parse without errors
scenarios.scenario-2: unpack → pack → unpack round-trip on minimal_valid_docx: part listing identical, every XML part tree-equal, every binary part byte-equal
scenarios.scenario-3: merge_runs collapses two adjacent w:r with identical w:rPr into one; never merges across w:hyperlink, w:bookmarkStart, w:bookmarkEnd, w:commentRangeStart, w:commentRangeEnd, w:commentReference, w:ins, w:del, w:fldChar, w:tab, w:br, w:sym, or any element introducing a non-text child between the runs
scenarios.scenario-4: escape_smart_quotes converts ASCII apostrophes to ’ in body text but skips runs with rStyle ending in 'Code'; idempotent on already-escaped input
scenarios.scenario-4b: escape_smart_quotes alternates double quotes per paragraph: first ASCII '"' → “, second → ”, third → “, etc. Unpaired (odd) double quote in a paragraph is left as ASCII '"'. Reset across paragraphs. Mixed apostrophes and double quotes counted independently.
scenarios.scenario-5: pack auto-repair: input with w:durableId=0xFFFFFFFE produces output with regenerated valid id; preserved_whitespace_runs increments for each w:t with leading/trailing whitespace lacking xml:space=preserve
scenarios.scenario-6: unpack(non-zip file) raises OOXML_NOT_A_ZIP
scenarios.scenario-8: validate_relationships on unpack tree with dangling Target raises OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN naming the missing target
scenarios.scenario-9: [Content_Types].xml Override entry order in pack output equals input order exactly

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never invoke LibreOffice, soffice, or any external converter
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never silently drop or rename a part during round-trip
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never double-escape an existing XML entity

V-M-OOXML.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure stdlib zipfile + lxml manipulation, deterministic, no LLM

scenarios.scenario-1: validate_plan(EditPlan with 1 replace_text op + valid anchor) passes
scenarios.scenario-2: validate_plan(EditPlan with empty ops) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-3: validate_plan(op with anchor.value containing ASCII control characters or exceeding 512 chars) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-4: edit(minimal_valid_docx, replace_text "Hello" → "Hi") produces output passing M-VALIDATE; before_snippet/after_snippet captured in diff
scenarios.scenario-5: resolve_anchor on text appearing twice without context_before/after raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS
scenarios.scenario-6: resolve_anchor on text not present raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-7: edit with insert_paragraph(style="Heading2") after first paragraph: output has new w:p with w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", validates clean
scenarios.scenario-8: tracked_replace op produces sibling w:del + w:ins with author/date attributes; w:rPr preserved on both branches
scenarios.scenario-9: tracked_delete on full paragraph also marks paragraph mark via w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del
scenarios.scenario-10: add_comment with parent_id=0 creates a top-level commentRangeStart/End pair around the target plus a sibling commentRangeStart/End pair for the reply; the reply linkage lives in word/commentsExtended.xml as w15:commentEx with paraIdParent matching the parent paraId; comments.xml carries both top-level and reply text; content types registered
scenarios.scenario-11: add_comment respects "commentRangeStart/End are siblings of w:r, never inside w:r" invariant — schema check on document.xml confirms
scenarios.scenario-12: accept_change on an insertion by id removes the w:ins wrapper and keeps inserted text; accept_change on a deletion by id removes the w:del subtree (deleted text is gone); reject_change on an insertion drops the w:ins subtree (inserted text gone); reject_change on a deletion removes the w:del wrapper and restores w:t from w:delText
scenarios.scenario-13: build_edit_prompt does not include any raw OOXML XML in the LLM-facing prompt — only extracted text + stable anchors + op schema
scenarios.scenario-14: execute_op on an op carrying an unsupported type (for example type="rotate_image" in v0) raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation
scenarios.scenario-15: edit run end-to-end: backup created, M-VALIDATE invoked on output, EditResult.success=True, diff.length == ops_total
scenarios.scenario-16: set_paragraph_style with style_id="Heading2" on body paragraph: output paragraph has w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", original w:rPr preserved on existing runs, M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-17: set_paragraph_style with style_id missing from styles.xml raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before mutation
scenarios.scenario-18: delete_paragraph on anchored paragraph removes the w:p subtree; relationships referenced only from that paragraph are removed from document.xml.rels; M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-19: paragraph_index anchors resolve against the original tree even after earlier ops in the same plan inserted or deleted paragraphs (no re-indexing)
scenarios.scenario-20: EditMetadata.source_prompt_hash is computed by M-EDIT (not read from model output) and is stable for identical inputs
scenarios.scenario-21: EditPlan with op-1 delete_paragraph(anchor=A) followed by op-2 anchored at A by paragraph_index/hash/text: op-2 raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND because the original-tree anchor for A no longer resolves after op-1 mutated the tree, surfacing the documented "no re-indexing across ops" semantics
scenarios.scenario-22: edit(severity_mode="strict") on a fixture that produces a soft violation in the output triggers EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED and refuses to write output_path; backup remains intact
scenarios.scenario-23: EditPlan JSON uses 'type' as the discriminator field on both EditOp and Anchor (not 'op_type' and not 'kind') — schema test asserts no other discriminator name appears in the schema
scenarios.scenario-24: resolve_anchor with type='hash' on a tree where two different paragraphs share the same 8-hex SHA1 prefix raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS; the runtime never silently picks the first match. Test mechanics: a real 8-hex SHA1 collision is impractical to brute-force, so the test monkeypatches mint.edit._paragraph_hash (or equivalent helper) to return a fixed value 'deadbeef' for two distinct paragraphs in a fresh fixture; the assertion is that resolve_anchor raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS with both paragraph_index values listed in the error message.
scenarios.scenario-25: edit when the backup destination is unwritable (read-only directory or permission denied) raises BACKUP_FAILED before any unpack or tree mutation; the original document is unchanged on disk
scenarios.scenario-26: edit with output_path omitted writes to '<input-stem>.edited<ext>' next to the input; backup is at '<input>.bak'; output_path != backup_path
scenarios.scenario-27: edit on an EditPlan with format='pptx' raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation (PPTX edits are deferred to v0.5)
scenarios.scenario-28: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP exactly once and strictly before BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK; the backup file exists on disk with identical bytes to the original input
scenarios.scenario-29: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT exactly once and strictly before the first BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR; the rendered prompt for an LLM-driven plan is stable for identical input + anchor list

required-log-markers.marker-7: [Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE] (conditional — only when at least one op type is in {tracked_replace, tracked_delete, accept_change, reject_change})
required-log-markers.trace-sequence: BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP -> BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK -> BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT -> BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR(per op) -> BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP(per op, may emit BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE) -> BLOCK_OOXML_PACK -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never expose raw OOXML XML to the LLM in build_edit_prompt
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never apply operations if validate_plan failed
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never write output if M-VALIDATE on the result fails in strict mode without surfacing EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Must never modify tracked-change entries authored by other users beyond the documented per-id accept_change/reject_change ops
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: Must never proceed when anchor resolution is ambiguous; must raise EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS

V-M-EDIT.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — depends on external LLM for plan generation, but plan is typed JSON and every op is validated and bounded; backup is mandatory before any tree mutation

module-checks.check-1: python -c "import mint_python; import mint_python.core, mint_python.sdk, mint_python.execution, mint_python.rules, mint_python.grace, mint_python.mcp, mint_python.cli, mint_python.tests"

scenarios.scenario-1: All 9 entry points (top-level + 8 subpackages) import without error; exit 0
scenarios.scenario-2: mint_python.__version__ == "0.0.0"
scenarios.scenario-3: mint_python and every subpackage are listed in `unzip -l dist/*.whl` after `uv build`. Phase-level only — module-level workers do not run uv build per change.
scenarios.scenario-4: Each subpackage __init__.py contains the repo's `# FILE:` header convention (grep `^# FILE: src/mint_python/`). Forbids drift to one-line-docstring style.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: No subpackage may export a public function/class in Phase 0 — contracts populate in Phases 1-5; an early export risks contract drift before code lands
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: __init__.py files must not import from src/mint/* — the skeleton stays decoupled from the legacy package
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: PYTHON-FIRST POLICY (constraint-8 in requirements.xml): no MP-* module may invoke Node.js — no `subprocess` calls into `node`/`npm`/`vm2`/`isolated-vm`, no IPC bridges into `src/mint/sandbox/runner.js`, no shims that wrap docx-js. Allowed external processes from MP-* code: HTTP services (Gotenberg in Phase-5) and the future RestrictedPython sandbox (Phase-4). Enforcement command: `grep -rnE "(subprocess|os\.system|os\.popen|os\.spawn).*(node|npm|vm2|isolated-vm|src/mint/sandbox)" src/mint_python/ 2>/dev/null` returns zero matches; CI hook recommended.

V-MP-PKG.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — empty package skeleton; idempotent file creation, no shared mutable state, no env reads. A worker creating these files cannot interfere with another worker editing src/mint/ as long as both stay within their declared write scope (worker-owns / controller-owns split in development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy).

V-MP-FLAG.note: Cross-cuts M-CONFIG (Engine enum + field + parsing) and M-CLI (--engine arg + os.environ write + _select_engine chokepoint). Hosted as a separate V-* entry so a single failure packet maps cleanly to the feature; V-M-CONFIG and V-M-CLI cross-reference it via their test-files lists.

swarm-fixtures.fixture-1: Reuse the `clean_env` autouse fixture from tests/unit/test_config.py (copy or import). MUST extend its env_keys list with "MINT_ENGINE" before any test in this file runs. Without this extension, an os.environ write inside one test (e.g. via subprocess or main()) leaks into a sibling pytest test run in the same process.
swarm-fixtures.fixture-2: For subprocess scenarios use `subprocess.run([..., env={...minimal env without MINT_ENGINE..., **overrides}], capture_output=True)`. NEVER inherit os.environ — that defeats isolation across pytest workers when one worker pre-set the var.

scenarios.scenario-1: MintConfig with required env set, MINT_ENGINE unset → engine == Engine.PYTHON (default)
scenarios.scenario-2: monkeypatch.setenv("MINT_ENGINE", "js"); load_config() → engine == Engine.JS
scenarios.scenario-3: monkeypatch.setenv("MINT_ENGINE", "garbage"); load_config() raises ConfigInvalidError; message contains the literal substring "MINT_ENGINE" and lists both valid values "python" and "js"
scenarios.scenario-4: _select_engine(MintConfig(engine=Engine.JS)) returns "js" without raising
scenarios.scenario-5: _select_engine(MintConfig(engine=Engine.PYTHON)) raises NotImplementedError; message contains both "MINT_ENGINE=python" and "Phase 0"
scenarios.scenario-6: subprocess: env={MINT_ENGINE=python, ...required}, args=[python, -m, mint.cli, --engine, js, --help] → exit 0; stdout contains "--engine"
scenarios.scenario-7: FROZEN INVARIANT: cfg = load_config(); pytest.raises(dataclasses.FrozenInstanceError): cfg.engine = Engine.JS — proves the dataclass is frozen and the override channel is os.environ, not setattr
scenarios.scenario-8: ARG POSITION: subprocess `python -m mint.cli validate --engine js fixture.docx` (--engine AFTER subcommand) → non-zero exit, argparse usage error to stderr. Confirms --engine is top-level only and does not silently accept post-subcommand placement.
scenarios.scenario-9: CLI override precedence: subprocess with env MINT_ENGINE=python, args [..., --engine, js, validate, ...] → captured stderr/log shows BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG entry where engine resolved to "js", proving os.environ write in main() ran BEFORE config() was first called. (Asserts on the log payload, not on the side effect alone.)
scenarios.scenario-10: Default-engine path: subprocess with no MINT_ENGINE, no --engine, args [..., validate, fixture] → stderr contains BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG and BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE markers; does NOT contain BLOCK_DISPATCH or any [Validate]/[Create]/[Sandbox] block markers; exit non-zero with NotImplementedError surfaced.
scenarios.scenario-11: Env-isolation invariant: extend tests/unit/test_config.py::clean_env env_keys to include "MINT_ENGINE"; assert that running this V-MP-FLAG suite immediately followed by tests/unit/test_config.py with no isolation passes — i.e., no test in this file leaks MINT_ENGINE into os.environ for the next test.
scenarios.scenario-12: .ENV-FILE PRECEDENCE: write a temp .env containing `MINT_ENGINE=python`; with os.environ["MINT_ENGINE"]="js" set, call load_config(env_file=tmp) → engine == Engine.JS. Confirms os.environ wins over .env file (mirrors the existing dotenv_values + os.environ overlay pattern in load_config). Inverse case (no env override, only .env): engine == Engine.PYTHON from the .env value.
scenarios.scenario-13: PYPROJECT.TOML SHAPE: `python -c "import tomllib; d=tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb')); assert d['tool']['hatch']['build']['targets']['wheel']['packages']==['src/mint','src/mint_python']"` exits 0. Module-level guard catches typos in the packages list before phase-level uv build would.
scenarios.scenario-14: .ENV.EXAMPLE DOCS: `.env.example` contains a `# MINT_ENGINE=js` commented opt-in line; grep `-E "^# *MINT_ENGINE=" .env.example` matches exactly once. Forbids the file shipping without engine documentation when the default fail-closed is python.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [Config][load][BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG] — payload extended (Phase-6) to include resolved engine value at INFO
required-log-markers.marker-2: [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] — NEW; emitted on every non-help/non-usage-error main() before dispatch; INFO level

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: NotImplementedError raised on the js path
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: config() loaded before os.environ override is applied (CLI override silently dropped)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Any test in this file mutates os.environ without using monkeypatch.setenv (must be reverted automatically by pytest)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: Subprocess scenarios that inherit the parent os.environ for MINT_ENGINE — defeats parallel worker isolation

V-MP-FLAG.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE provided the clean_env fixture is extended (fixture-1) and subprocess scenarios pass explicit env (fixture-2). Without these, parallel test workers in the same process can leak MINT_ENGINE between tests via main()'s os.environ write, producing flaky failures. Process-level workers (pytest-xdist -n auto) are inherently safe because each worker is a fresh process. Document the in-process risk explicitly in the test file header so a future contributor (or fix-agent) does not strip the fixture.

V-MP-STYLE.schema-reference: docs/style-preset-schema.md (normative); presets must validate against the JSON Schema described there. The schema is the single source of truth for required keys, color hex format, palette tokens (@primary etc.), StyleSpec defaults, and SemVer versioning rules.

scenarios.scenario-1: Pt(12) returns 240 (twentieths of a point); Pt(0.5) returns 10
scenarios.scenario-2: ColorPalette('alga_corporate').resolve('primary') returns the documented hex (e.g. '#0F4C81'); unknown name raises KeyError naming the palette + missing color
scenarios.scenario-3: load_preset('alga_corporate') returns a Style namespace with heading1, heading2, body, table_header, caption all populated as frozen Style instances
scenarios.scenario-4: load_preset(path=tmp_json) loads from arbitrary file when JSON validates against schema
scenarios.scenario-5: load_preset('does_not_exist') raises STYLE_PRESET_NOT_FOUND naming the registry contents
scenarios.scenario-6: load_preset(path=invalid_schema_json) raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA; message contains a JSON Pointer (e.g. "/typography/heading1/color") AND the constraint name. Test parametrizes 4+ violations: missing required key (color_palette.primary), bad hex format ("rgb(...)"), wrong type (size_pt as string), wrong enum (alignment="diagonal").
scenarios.scenario-7: Style frozen invariant: pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError): style.color_hex = '#fff' — proves Style cannot be mutated post-construction
scenarios.scenario-8: load_preset emits [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] with name + source payload
scenarios.scenario-9: PALETTE TOKEN RESOLUTION: a StyleSpec carrying color="@primary" resolves through the preset's own color_palette during load_preset; the returned Style instance has color_hex="#0F4C81" (or whatever primary is). Round-trip: re-serializing must NOT replace the literal hex with the @token (loaders don't compress).
scenarios.scenario-10: DANGLING TOKEN: a preset whose StyleSpec uses color="@nonexistent" raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA pointing at the typography field that referenced the missing palette key; message names the missing token.
scenarios.scenario-11: DEFAULTS: a StyleSpec with only the three required fields (font, size_pt, color) loads without error and Style instance has bold=False, italic=False, alignment="left", spacing_before_pt=0, spacing_after_pt=0, line_height=1.15, keep_with_next=False — exactly the schema-documented defaults.
scenarios.scenario-12: VERSION MISMATCH: a preset with `"version": "2.0"` raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA naming "version" and the supported major (Phase-7 supports 1.x).
scenarios.scenario-13: REGISTRY INTEGRITY: at module import, all three built-in presets (alga_corporate, minimal, compact) validate against the schema. A pytest test enumerates `mint_python.sdk.presets` and calls load_preset on each; all return non-empty Style namespaces with the six required typography keys.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Style instance must remain frozen — mutating any field after construction is forbidden
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: load_preset must never return a partially-populated Style namespace — all required keys (heading1..caption) must be present or the call must raise
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SWARM-SAFETY: MP-STYLE must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton. The preset registry IS module-level, but it is read-only; load_preset(path=...) returns a fresh Style namespace WITHOUT updating the registry. Enforcement: `python -c "import mint_python.sdk; r = mint_python.sdk.presets; r2 = mint_python.sdk.presets; assert r is r2 and frozenset(r) == frozenset(['alga_corporate','minimal','compact'])"` exits 0 in any test order.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: load_preset MUST NOT silently fall through to a partial Style on schema violation — raising is mandatory; this prevents downstream MP-CONTENT/MP-TABLE renders against undefined Style fields.

V-MP-STYLE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure deterministic; no I/O beyond JSON file reads in tmp; no env reads; module-level state is read-only registry

scenarios.scenario-1: Paragraph('hello').render(doc) appends a w:p element with a single w:r containing 'hello'
scenarios.scenario-2: Paragraph fluent: Paragraph('a').add_run('b', style=bold_style) returns self; renders two w:r with distinct rPr
scenarios.scenario-3: Paragraph inherits Style.body when no per-run style provided
scenarios.scenario-4: Image.from_path(tmp_png).render(doc) produces a w:drawing with a unique rId; pillow loads the image to compute aspect ratio
scenarios.scenario-5: Image.from_path('/nonexistent.png') raises IMAGE_FILE_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-6: Image.from_bytes(b'\x00bad', format='png') raises IMAGE_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED OR a pillow-derived error wrapped with the path/format context
scenarios.scenario-7: Image width=Inches(3) sets the OOXML extent in EMU correctly (3 * 914400)
scenarios.scenario-9: add_run(text, bold=True) on a paragraph styled with body (bold=False) emits a run whose font.bold is True; surrounding runs inherit body unchanged
scenarios.scenario-10: add_run(text, italic=True, color='#FF0000', font_size_pt=18.0) emits a run with italic + color FF0000 + size 18pt; the unstyled neighbor inherits the body Style intact
scenarios.scenario-11: add_run(text, underline=True) emits an underlined run; runs without an underline override are not underlined (Style has no underline field, so None means no underline)
scenarios.scenario-12: Per-run override of bool=False beats Style.bold=True: explicit Run.bold=False wins over inherited heading1 Style.bold=True
scenarios.scenario-13: Paragraph.add_run kwargs (bold/italic/underline/color/font_size_pt) reach the resulting Run instance via construction unchanged
scenarios.scenario-14: Run(text, color='red') and Run(text, color='#GGHHII') raise ValueError mentioning '#RRGGBB' or 'hex digits'
scenarios.scenario-15: Run(text, font_size_pt=0) and Run(text, font_size_pt=-1.5) raise ValueError mentioning '> 0'
scenarios.scenario-16: Run(text, link='https://...').render emits a w:hyperlink wrapping the w:r; the hyperlink carries an r:id pointing to a package relationship with target_ref=URL and is_external=True
scenarios.scenario-17: Run(text, link='#anchor').render emits a w:hyperlink with w:anchor='anchor' (no package relationship — internal cross-ref)
scenarios.scenario-18: Run(text, bookmark='name').render emits w:bookmarkStart + w:bookmarkEnd siblings around the w:r (or hyperlink wrapper if also present); ids match between start and end
scenarios.scenario-19: Run carrying both link and bookmark: bookmarkStart, hyperlink (containing w:r), bookmarkEnd appear as flat siblings of w:p in document order
scenarios.scenario-20: Multiple bookmarks within one paragraph or across consecutive renders into the same Document receive distinct ids; the allocator scans the document body for the current max bookmarkStart/@w:id
scenarios.scenario-21: Run(text, link='') / link=' ' / link='#1starts_with_digit' / link='#has-dash' raise ValueError
scenarios.scenario-22: Run(text, bookmark='') / bookmark='2nd_anchor' / bookmark='has space' raise ValueError
scenarios.scenario-23: Paragraph('Section\tPage', tab_stops=[TabStop(6.0, RIGHT, DOTS)]) emits exactly one paragraph_format.tab_stops entry at Inches(6) with WD_TAB_ALIGNMENT.RIGHT and WD_TAB_LEADER.DOTS; the literal '\\t' becomes a w:tab element so text after it is right-aligned at the stop
scenarios.scenario-24: Multiple TabStop entries are applied in order; paragraph_format.tab_stops yields len == N
scenarios.scenario-25: TabStop(position_inches=0) and TabStop(position_inches=-1.5) raise ValueError mentioning '> 0'
scenarios.scenario-26: Paragraph with default empty tab_stops list does not allocate any python-docx tab_stops collection entries
scenarios.scenario-27: First Run with footnote='text' bootstraps a /word/footnotes.xml package part with the canonical separator (id=-1) + continuationSeparator (id=0) entries; the user footnote lives at id=1 with the supplied body text
scenarios.scenario-28: N consecutive footnoted runs share one footnotes part; user footnote ids increment 1, 2, ..., N; matching number of w:footnoteReference elements appear in the document body, each pointing to a distinct id
scenarios.scenario-29: Run(text, footnote='') and Run(text, footnote=' ') raise ValueError mentioning 'non-empty'

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Content][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT] — fires per Paragraph or Image .render() call

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Paragraph or Image must NEVER write to the filesystem during render — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Run must not be re-used across Paragraphs (would corrupt OOXML). Render() may consume the Run; subsequent renders raise.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Per-run overrides set to None must NOT clobber the inherited Style. Only an explicit non-None override value reaches python-docx font properties.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Bookmark id allocator must scan the entire body for max existing id before emitting; never restart from 0 mid-document.

V-MP-CONTENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1','2']]).render(doc) creates 2 rows x 2 cols; first row treated as header when header=True (default)
scenarios.scenario-2: Table.from_markdown('| a | b |\n|---|---|\n| 1 | 2 |') produces equivalent table
scenarios.scenario-3: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1,'b':2},{'a':3,'b':4}]) emits 3 rows x 2 cols (header + 2 data rows); column order follows insertion order of first dict
scenarios.scenario-4: Table.financial(rows) right-aligns numeric cells AND applies thousands separator (1000 -> '1,000')
scenarios.scenario-5: Table.comparison(left='Q1', right='Q2', rows=[...]) produces a 2-column table with left/right headers
scenarios.scenario-6: apply_style(preset_table) overrides cell style; set_column_widths([2,3]) sets column widths in inches; autofit=True sets the python-docx table autofit flag
scenarios.scenario-7: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1']]) raises TABLE_RAGGED_ROWS naming the bad row index
scenarios.scenario-8: Table.from_markdown('not a markdown table') raises TABLE_MARKDOWN_PARSE_ERROR
scenarios.scenario-9: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1},{'b':2}]) raises TABLE_INVALID_DICT_KEYS naming the diverging keys (rows must share the same key set)
scenarios.scenario-10: Cell(value, colspan=N) on a row with N+ columns merges the primary cell with the next N-1 cells; merged python-docx cells share a single _tc element; top-left content survives (covered cells' content is silently dropped per python-docx merge semantics)
scenarios.scenario-11: Cell(value, rowspan=M) on row r merges (r,c) with (r+M-1,c); python-docx _tc identity test confirms the merge
scenarios.scenario-12: Cell(value, rowspan=M, colspan=N) merges the M x N rectangle; all M*N python-docx cells share the same _tc; cells outside the region remain independent
scenarios.scenario-13: Cell(value, colspan=0) or Cell(value, rowspan=-1) raises TABLE_MERGE_INVALID_SPAN at construction
scenarios.scenario-14: Cell(rowspan=2) on the last row, or Cell(colspan=N+1) at column c with c+N >= col_count, raises TABLE_MERGE_OUT_OF_BOUNDS at render time naming the offending grid coordinates

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Table][render][BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE] — fires once per Table.render() call; payload: row_count, col_count

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Factories must validate row shape before constructing Table — never return a partially-built Table that would raise mid-render
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: financial() must not silently coerce non-numeric cells; non-numerics keep their raw value and skip thousands separator
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Render-time merge validation must NEVER produce malformed OOXML — out-of-bounds spans must raise BEFORE writing the merge, leaving the in-progress python-docx Table in a state the caller can discard

V-MP-TABLE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction + render onto per-test Document; no shared state

scenarios.scenario-1: Section('Title', level=1).add_paragraph('text') returns self (fluent chain works)
scenarios.scenario-2: Section.render(doc) emits a w:p with heading style matching level (Heading 1, Heading 2, ...) followed by the content blocks in order
scenarios.scenario-3: add_paragraph accepts both str (shorthand) and Paragraph instance
scenarios.scenario-4: Section('X', level=10) raises SECTION_LEVEL_OUT_OF_RANGE (valid: 1..6)
scenarios.scenario-5: (Phase-8 unstub commit caf30ab) Section('X', level=1).add_chart(chart: Chart) appends Chart to _blocks AND returns self for fluent chaining. Pre-Phase-8 raised NotImplementedError + emitted BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD; that stub-era contract is retired. NOTE: Section.add_chart with a non-Chart input may raise TypeError via mypy/runtime checks but the legacy "Phase 2" message is no longer expected.
scenarios.scenario-6: (Phase-8) Section.render walks Chart.render — saving a Section that contains a Chart produces an inline_shape whose width.emu == round(width_inches × 914400). The legacy BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD-on-add_chart trace from Phase-7 is retired; Chart blocks emit their own [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] marker via the per-block render dispatch (no Section-side marker on add_chart anymore).

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] — fires on stub touch; payload: method + target_phase

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: add_chart must raise immediately after emitting BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD — never silently no-op
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Fluent methods must NEVER mutate other Section instances — each .add_* mutates self only

V-MP-SECTION.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction and per-test render

scenarios.scenario-1: Callout(text, kind=INFO).render emits a 1x1 python-docx Table; cell shading fill = "EBF5FB"; cell left border color = "2E75B6"; default title "Info" appears in cell text
scenarios.scenario-2: Callout(text, kind=WARNING).render uses the amber palette: shading "FFF8E1", left border "E8A838"; default title "Warning"
scenarios.scenario-3: Callout(text, kind=CODE).render uses grey palette ("F5F5F5" shading, "DDDDDD" border) and switches the body run's font to Courier New
scenarios.scenario-4: Explicit title kwarg overrides the kind default — the default title is NOT emitted when title is provided
scenarios.scenario-5: render emits exactly one [MP-Callout][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CALLOUT] marker carrying kind and has_title
scenarios.scenario-6: Section.add_callout(callout) appends to _blocks, returns self; Section.render walks Callout.render so the callout's table appears in document order

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Callout][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CALLOUT] — fires once per Callout.render call; payload: kind, has_title

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: render() must NEVER write to the filesystem — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: The python-docx-seeded empty paragraph in the new cell must be removed before our managed paragraphs are added — leaving it produces a leading blank line above the title

V-MP-CALLOUT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: List(['a','b','c']).render(doc) emits 3 paragraphs each with python-docx 'List Bullet' style and the corresponding text
scenarios.scenario-2: List(items, kind=ListKind.NUMBERED).render emits paragraphs with 'List Number' style
scenarios.scenario-3: List(items, kind=ListKind.CHECKLIST).render prefixes each item text with the unicode ☐ + space; underlying paragraph style is 'List Bullet' (no native checkbox content-control in v0.1)
scenarios.scenario-4: List(items=[]).render emits zero paragraphs (empty lists are silently allowed for conditional rendering)
scenarios.scenario-5: List(items, level=N) sets paragraph_format.left_indent to Inches(0.25 * N) for each emitted paragraph
scenarios.scenario-6: List(items, level=-1) raises LIST_LEVEL_OUT_OF_RANGE at construction time
scenarios.scenario-7: render emits exactly one [MP-List][render][BLOCK_RENDER_LIST] marker carrying kind, items count, and level
scenarios.scenario-8: Section.add_list(list_block) appends to _blocks, returns self; Section.render walks List.render so list paragraphs appear in document order

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-List][render][BLOCK_RENDER_LIST] — fires once per List.render call; payload: kind, items count, level

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: render() must NEVER write to the filesystem — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Mutating List.items between renders must NOT corrupt previously rendered paragraphs (python-docx materializes immediately on add_paragraph)

V-MP-LIST.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: Document(format='docx', title='X').with_style_preset('alga_corporate') returns self
scenarios.scenario-2: add_cover/add_section/add_toc/set_header/set_footer all return self for chaining; stored in declared order
scenarios.scenario-3: save(tmp_path / 'out.docx') produces a file that exists; lxml parses word/document.xml without error
scenarios.scenario-4: Document(format='pdf') raises DOCUMENT_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED naming valid formats {'docx'} for Phase-1
scenarios.scenario-5: Document(...).with_style_preset('does_not_exist') raises DOCUMENT_PRESET_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-6: All former stubs are now concrete: validate → ValidationReport, fix → FixReport, inject_grace → GRACEManifest, to_pdf → Path. Zero NotImplementedError raisers. Zero BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD emitters.
scenarios.scenario-7: save() emits exactly one [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] with section_count, table_count, output_path payload
scenarios.scenario-8: IDEMPOTENCY UNDER M-FINGERPRINT: invoking save() twice on the same Document produces two files whose `mint.fingerprint.fingerprint(path).hash` are equal. Pytest snippet (lives in tests/unit/test_mp_document.py): ``` from mint.fingerprint import fingerprint doc = build_doc(...) doc.save(tmp_path/"a.docx"); doc.save(tmp_path/"b.docx") assert fingerprint(tmp_path/"a.docx").hash == fingerprint(tmp_path/"b.docx").hash ``` Byte-equality is NOT asserted — python-docx writes attributes in non-deterministic order; the M-FINGERPRINT structural hash is the canonical equivalence relation. Failure mode: if hashes differ across two save() calls of the same Document, save() carries hidden non-determinism (e.g. uuid in cover, timestamp in header) — the test points at the diverging part by re-running with fingerprint().xml_sources to isolate which part of the ZIP changed.
scenarios.scenario-9: AUDIT-BASELINE PIN (Wave-7-5 e2e shape; mirrored here for module-local sanity): the golden 3-section document built per VF-013 produces a structural fingerprint that matches `tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json::structural_fingerprint_sha256`. First-ever run writes the baseline (with explicit `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` env, gated to prevent accidental overwrites). Subsequent runs assert equality; on mismatch the failure prints the new vs. old hash AND the diff of fingerprint().xml_sources to localize the regression.

required-log-markers.marker-2: RETIRED post-Phase-12 — BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD no longer fires; all Document stubs concrete

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: save() must never produce a partial file: on serialization error, the temp output is removed before re-raising
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX firing more than once for a single save() call is forbidden (would indicate retry without state cleanup)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SWARM-SAFETY: MP-DOCUMENT must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton. Document instances are explicit per-call; no `_default_document` or registry. Tests MUST use tmp_path for every save target — never write to a stable repo path. Enforcement: `grep -rn 'output\.docx\|/tmp/[a-z]*\.docx' tests/unit/test_mp_document.py tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py` returns zero matches outside tmp_path fixtures.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: save() output must NEVER include GRACE Custom XML Parts in Phase-7 (those are Phase-5/MP-GRACE territory). VF-013 inv-3 enforces this; a regression here would silently break the Phase-5 contract.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: NON-DETERMINISM: save() must NOT introduce timestamps, UUIDs, or random IDs into the output beyond what python-docx itself emits at fixed values. If python-docx ever starts emitting per-call timestamps (e.g. for core.xml created/modified fields), MP-DOCUMENT MUST override these to a fixed sentinel value to preserve idempotency under M-FINGERPRINT.

field.name: schema_version
field.description: Baseline format version; bumped on shape changes

field.name: committed_at
field.description: ISO date of baseline commit; informational

field.name: generator
field.description: Tool versions used to generate baseline

field.name: document_spec
field.description: Reference to the function that builds the golden doc; future agents must rebuild via this same function

field.name: xml_sources
field.description: Parts contributing to the fingerprint hash; recorded so a future drift can be localized. NOTE: M-FINGERPRINT for python-docx output yields the styles.xml + numbering.xml subset shown here — NOT the docx-js-era list (document.xml + styles.xml + theme1.xml). See tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json for the canonical Phase-7 baseline value.

field.name: counts
field.description: Structural counts from BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX payload + lxml introspection

field.name: audit_violations
field.description: M-VALIDATE audit-mode violations grouped by rule_id+severity (NOT individual instances — instances drift across python-docx versions; aggregated counts are stable)

field.name: hard_violation_count
field.description: MUST be 0 for VF-013 lenient acceptance; tracked separately for fast-fail

field.name: schema_validation
field.description: lxml_parses asserts word/document.xml parses without raising; ecma376_validated=false for Phase-7 (full ECMA-376 schema validation deferred to Phase-3)

baseline-update-protocol.step-1: Set `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` in the test invocation env to allow overwrite (default is read-only assertion).
baseline-update-protocol.step-2: Manually inspect the diff: structural_fingerprint_sha256 change WITHOUT a corresponding intentional MP-* change is a regression flag, not a baseline update.
baseline-update-protocol.step-3: Commit the new baseline in the same PR that ships the corresponding MP-* code change; commit message MUST reference the rationale (e.g. "MP-DOCUMENT: switch heading rendering from Title to Heading 1; baseline rotated").
baseline-update-protocol.step-4: Audit-violation rotation: a baseline that grows hard_violation_count above 0 is a Phase-7 acceptance failure — no rotation; fix the regression.

V-MP-DOCUMENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — save() targets tmp_path per test; no shared mutable state. e2e wave (Wave-7-5) reads-only against M-VALIDATE; baseline assertion is deterministic. Worker-write to tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json is gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env so concurrent workers cannot race-overwrite.

implementation-path.source: src/mint_python/core/chart.py — Chart class definition + 7 factories + from_matplotlib/seaborn/plotly + render. The class lives in CORE per repo convention (Phase-7 established this with Document/Section/Table/Style/Image). MP-SDK re-exports Chart in src/mint_python/sdk/__init__.py (Phase-8 Wave-8-2 step-3); users write `from mint_python.sdk import Chart` but the class identity is `mint_python.core.chart.Chart`.
implementation-path.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (legacy js engine — V-MP-PKG forbidden-2 invariant); src/mint_python/sdk/__init__.py (Wave-8-2 worker scope, NOT Wave-8-1); any other src/mint_python/core/* file (Phase-8 doesn't modify sibling modules).

scenarios.scenario-1: Chart.bar(labels, values, title, width_inches) returns a Chart instance whose chart_type=='bar', _png_bytes is non-empty, and width_inches matches input
scenarios.scenario-2: Chart.line(labels, values, title, width_inches) — same shape verification with chart_type=='line'
scenarios.scenario-3: Chart.stacked_bar(labels, series_dict, ...) accepts dict-of-series shape and produces stacked rendering (verify via matplotlib axes inspection if feasible OR PNG bytes non-empty + chart_type tag)
scenarios.scenario-4: Chart.pie(labels, values, ...) — verify wedge count matches len(values) via figure introspection before render closes
scenarios.scenario-5: Chart.heatmap(matrix, row_labels, col_labels, ...) — matrix shape mismatch with labels raises CHART_INVALID_DATA
scenarios.scenario-6: Chart.waterfall(labels, deltas, ...) — verify sum-of-deltas annotation present (composed bar primitive)
scenarios.scenario-7: Chart.gantt(tasks, ...) — verify horizontal-bar shape; tasks=[(name, start, duration), ...] minimum schema
scenarios.scenario-8: Chart.from_matplotlib(fig, caption, width_inches, vector=True) wraps user Figure; vector kwarg accepted and stored on instance but Phase-8 PNG-only — assert _png_bytes non-empty + caption attached
scenarios.scenario-9: Chart.from_seaborn(fig, **kwargs) — when seaborn installed, aliases to from_matplotlib; when seaborn ImportError, raises ImportError with "pip install seaborn" hint. Test parametrized with monkeypatch on sys.modules to simulate the missing-seaborn case.
scenarios.scenario-10: (Phase-12 unstub commit 7ca3ec3) Chart.from_plotly(plotly_fig) calls fig.to_image(format="png", scale=2) via the kaleido backend; returns Chart with chart_type="plotly" and non-empty _png_bytes. Pre-Phase-12 raised NotImplementedError + emitted BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD; that stub-era contract is retired.
scenarios.scenario-10b: When plotly is missing from sys.modules, Chart.from_plotly raises ImportError naming "plotly kaleido" install hint. When plotly imports OK but fig.to_image fails (kaleido absent), raises ImportError pointing at kaleido. Both error paths surface as ImportError, NOT NotImplementedError — the Phase-9+ stub semantics are gone.
scenarios.scenario-11: Each factory emits [MP-Chart][<factory_name>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] exactly once with chart_type and data_shape payload — assert via marker_counter and explicit caplog inspection
scenarios.scenario-12: Chart.render(parent_doc) emits [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] chart_type=<name> emu_width=<int> exactly once — verify emu_width = round(width_inches × 914400)
scenarios.scenario-13: Chart.render produces python-docx inline_shape with width.emu == round(width_inches × 914400) (asserted on a fresh DocxDocument)
scenarios.scenario-14: CORPORATE THEMING: build a Chart under alga_corporate preset; matplotlib rcParams snapshot before/after the build call shows font.family and color cycle changes (or, if rcParams not exposed, assert that the active palette resolved to alga primary hex via figure axis line color)
scenarios.scenario-15: CHARTJUNK: rendered Chart's matplotlib axes have spines.top.set_visible(False) and spines.right.set_visible(False) — verify by retaining a hook to the figure or by snapshot of figure XML before close
scenarios.scenario-16: CHART_INVALID_DATA: Chart.bar(labels=["a","b"], values=[1.0]) raises with message naming the length mismatch (len(labels)=2, len(values)=1)

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Chart][<factory>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] — fires per factory call (one per: bar, line, stacked_bar, pie, heatmap, waterfall, gantt) AND once per from_matplotlib/from_seaborn call (chart_type captured from method name)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Chart construction must NEVER write to filesystem — PNG bytes are cached in-memory in the Chart instance
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: matplotlib backend MUST be 'Agg' at import time — Chart module forces matplotlib.use('Agg') before any Figure construction (no display dep)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SEABORN MUST NOT be imported at module level — only inside Chart.from_seaborn lazy-import. Verify with `python -c "import mint_python.core.chart; assert 'seaborn' not in sys.modules"`
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Plotly stub MUST raise BEFORE any matplotlib invocation — never returns a partially-built Chart instance
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: NON-REGRESSION: Chart module must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton (matplotlib rcParams modification at module load is forbidden — themes apply per-construct via context manager OR per-call rcParams override that does not leak)

V-MP-CHART.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES: matplotlib backend is process-local (Agg forced); rcParams modifications inside `with mpl.rc_context(...):` blocks don't leak across tests; tmp_path mandate for any image writes; seaborn lazy-import keeps the dep optional. Mid-risk due to matplotlib-version variance — pin matplotlib>=3.7 to stabilize rcParams behavior.

scenarios.scenario-1: All §3 names import: `from mint_python.sdk import Document, Section, Table, Style, Image, TOC, Pt, ColorPalette, presets` exits 0
scenarios.scenario-2: presets registry contains exactly the agreed Phase-1 entries: 'alga_corporate', 'minimal', 'compact'; presets['alga_corporate'] is a path-or-dict resolvable by load_preset
scenarios.scenario-3: with_style_from(tmp_json) on a Document instance loads the preset and returns self for chaining
scenarios.scenario-4: SDK module re-exports do NOT shadow underlying modules — `mint_python.sdk.Document is mint_python.core.document.Document`

required-log-markers.marker-1: (none — SDK is a re-export module; downstream modules log)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SDK must NOT export anything beyond the §3 surface — extras risk divergence from handover spec
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: SDK must NOT import from src/mint/* (legacy) — the skeleton stays decoupled per V-MP-PKG forbidden-2

V-MP-SDK.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure re-export + small registry; no I/O

V-MP-RULES.source: src/mint_python/rules/ — pure Python successor to M-RULES (src/mint/rules/engine.py). Replaces the Phase-6 empty skeleton with concrete Rule/Violation types, YAML loader, XPath evaluate engine, and all_rules registry.
V-MP-RULES.note: Shared YAML rule files live at rules/d-hard.yaml, rules/d-soft.yaml, rules/p-hard.yaml — same path M-RULES reads. MP-RULES does NOT duplicate these files; it reads the same YAML. The ALLOWED cross-package dependency from src/mint_python/rules/ to rules/ is a documented exception to forbidden-2 (V-MP-PKG constraint-8 / V-MP-STYLE forbidden-2), justified by the shared-config contract that avoids YAML duplication across engines.
V-MP-RULES.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (forbidden-2: no legacy js-engine imports); src/mint_python/core/* (MP-RULES is a leaf utility with no MP-* production deps); docs/*.xml (controller-owned); tests/unit/conftest.py; tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SILENT-XPATH-ERROR: evaluate() raises an uncaught lxml XPathError when given an invalid XPath — forbidden. Must catch etree.XPathError (any type: syntax, namespace, type error) and log at WARNING with rule_id + xpath, then return None.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: RULE-DUPLICATION: hardcoded rule definitions inside source (vs. loaded from YAML). The single YAML source of truth at rules/*.yaml is shared between M-RULES and MP-RULES.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: NO-LOG-ON-FAILURE: all_rules() swallows a broken YAML file without raising RuleLoadError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: SCALAR-RESULT-PASSTHROUGH: evaluate() returns a scalar (number, string, bool) from XPath evaluation instead of wrapping it in a list before passing to check logic. Must normalize tree.xpath() result via isinstance check (list→keep, else→[result]).
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: UNPROTECTED-ALL_RULES: all_rules() reads a YAML file without catching yaml.YAMLError — leaks raw parse exception instead of raising RuleLoadError.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Rules][evaluate][BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE] — fires once per evaluate() call; payload: rule_id, check kind. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE / # END_BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
required-log-markers.marker-2: [MP-Rules][all_rules][BLOCK_LOAD_RULES] — fires once per all_rules() call; payload: doc_format, rule_count. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES / # END_BLOCK_LOAD_RULES.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.rules import all_rules; r = all_rules(doc_format='docx'); assert len(r) > 0"
module-checks.check-5: python -c "from mint_python.rules import all_rules; r = all_rules(doc_format='pptx'); assert len(r) > 0"
module-checks.check-6: python -c "import mint_python.rules; assert all(n in dir(mint_python.rules) for n in ['Rule','Violation','Severity','FixCategory','evaluate','all_rules','classify_severity','get_hint'])"

scenarios.scenario-1: YAML loading DOCX: all_rules(doc_format='docx') returns list[Rule] from d-hard.yaml + d-soft.yaml; each Rule has id, format='docx', severity, xpath, check, fix_category, hint, description. Load from rules/ directory (same path as M-RULES).
scenarios.scenario-2: YAML loading PPTX: all_rules(doc_format='pptx') returns list[Rule] from p-hard.yaml. D-H* rules excluded.
scenarios.scenario-3: evaluate 'exists': rule D-H03 (xpath=//w:tblW[@w:type='pct'], check=exists) on docx containing percentage-width table returns Violation. On clean docx returns None (false-positive guard).
scenarios.scenario-4: evaluate 'count_gt_zero': rule P-H03 (xpath=//p:sp[...], check=count_gt_zero) on slide XML with shapes → Violation; on slide with zero matching elements → None.
scenarios.scenario-5: evaluate 'tbl_width_mismatch': D-H01 on bad_column_widths.docx → Violation. On minimal_valid.docx → None. Edge cases: table missing tblGrid → None; table with w:type='auto' → None; negative gridCol/@w values → filtered per lstrip('-').isdigit().
scenarios.scenario-6: evaluate 'sum_mismatch': on XML with mismatched sum → Violation; matching sum → None. Non-numeric text values in sum elements → None (ValueError/TypeError caught, no raise).
scenarios.scenario-7: XPath error: evaluate() with invalid xpath (syntax, namespace, type error) logs [MP-Rules][evaluate][BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE] at WARNING with rule_id + xpath, returns None (never raises). Mirrors M-RULES engine.py:103-108.
scenarios.scenario-8: evaluate 'exists' negative on PPTX: P-H02 (xpath=//a:rPr/a:latin[contains(@typeface,'Times')], check=exists) on bad_font.pptx → Violation. On minimal_valid.pptx → None.
scenarios.scenario-9: classify_severity + get_hint: classify_severity(Violation(severity=HARD, fix_category=DESTRUCTIVE)) → (HARD, DESTRUCTIVE); classify_severity(Violation(severity=SOFT, fix_category=VISUAL)) → (SOFT, VISUAL). get_hint('D-H01') returns dict with rule/reason/fix_instruction; get_hint('UNKNOWN-999') returns dict with reason='Unknown rule'.
scenarios.scenario-10: Trace: all_rules() emits BLOCK_LOAD_RULES at INFO with doc_format + rule_count. evaluate() emits BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE at INFO (per-call, for every rule evaluated regardless of violation result — new contract for MP-RULES, distinct from M-RULES WARNING-only on error). XPath errors log at WARNING with BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
scenarios.scenario-11: Broken YAML: all_rules(rules_dir=dir_with_broken_yaml) raises RuleLoadError with the file path.
scenarios.scenario-12: Missing rules key: YAML with no 'rules' top-level key raises RuleLoadError.
scenarios.scenario-13: Empty rules dir: all_rules(rules_dir=empty_dir) returns empty list — no error.
scenarios.scenario-14: evaluate result wrapping: evaluate() must wrap non-list XPath results (e.g. number, string) via isinstance check → [result]. Mirrors M-RULES engine.py:110.

V-MP-RULES.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure deterministic utility; no mutable state; read-only shared rules/*.yaml. No MP-* production deps.

V-MP-VALIDATE.source: src/mint_python/validate.py — pure Python successor to M-VALIDATE (src/mint/validate.py). Validation engine: opens .docx zip, parses word/document.xml via lxml, runs all_rules, classifies violations, produces ValidationReport.
V-MP-VALIDATE.note: MP-VALIDATE works on saved .docx files (zip + lxml path), NOT on in-memory python-docx Document objects. This mirrors M-VALIDATE's approach and guarantees that validation checks the final serialized OOXML output. The temp-file delegation pattern (Document.validate → save temp → MP-VALIDATE.validate) is the integration bridge at Wave-9-4.
V-MP-VALIDATE.depends: MP-RULES (for Rule, Violation, Severity, FixCategory, evaluate, all_rules, get_hint)
V-MP-VALIDATE.forbidden-paths: src/mint/* (forbidden-2); mint.config (MP-VALIDATE defines its own SeverityMode enum — decoupled from legacy M-CONFIG); docs/*.xml; tests/unit/conftest.py; tests/unit/_mp_helpers.py.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SILENT-PARSE-FAILURE: _open_document_xml on broken zip or missing word/document.xml returns None — forbidden. Must raise InvalidDocumentError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: WILDCARD-EXCEPTION-CATCH: broad except swallowing parse errors without re-raise.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: NO-LOG-ON-REPORT: run_checks() produces report with no INFO log of mode + counts.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] — fires once per run_checks(); payload: mode, violations, hard_count, soft_count, passed. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / # END_BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.validate import validate; r = validate('tests/fixtures/minimal_valid.docx'); assert r.passed"
module-checks.check-5: python -c "from mint_python.validate import validate; r = validate('tests/fixtures/bad_column_widths.docx'); assert len([v for v in r.violations if v.rule_id == 'D-H01']) >= 1"

scenarios.scenario-1: AUDIT always passes: validate(minimal_valid.docx) → report.passed=True regardless of violations. The report contains violations but AUDIT mode is observational only.
scenarios.scenario-2: LENIENT mode: clean doc → passed=True. bad_column_widths.docx (D-H01 HARD) → passed=False. Soft violations in clean doc → still passed=True (LENIENT tolerates SOFT).
scenarios.scenario-3: STRICT mode: clean doc → passed=True. Any violation (hard or soft) → passed=False.
scenarios.scenario-4: ValidationReport properties: hard_violations, soft_violations filter correctly; total matches len(violations).
scenarios.scenario-5: classify_violations: groups by severity+fix_category. Empty violations list → all groups empty (no KeyError).
scenarios.scenario-6: Invalid document: run_checks() catches InvalidDocumentError from _open_document_xml and returns ValidationReport with XML-001 pseudo-rule violation (passed=False, hard_count=1). The top-level validate() returns this report — does NOT propagate the exception to caller. This matches legacy M-VALIDATE contract. Document not found, bad ZIP, broken XML inside — all three failure modes produce XML-001 report.
scenarios.scenario-7: Trace: run_checks() emits BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS at INFO before returning. Payload: mode, violations count, hard_count, soft_count, passed boolean.
scenarios.scenario-8: Golden doc lenient: validate(golden_doc.docx) → passed=True, hard_count=0.
scenarios.scenario-9: No matching rules for format: validate(pptx_file) with rules_dir containing only D-H* rules → 0 violations, passed=True.
scenarios.scenario-10: PPTX validation with matching rules: validate(minimal_valid.pptx) with rules_dir including p-hard.yaml → passed=True (AUDIT). validate(bad_font.pptx) → report contains P-H02 violation (non-embedded system font). _get_main_xml_path("pptx") returns "ppt/slides/slide1.xml".
scenarios.scenario-11: Format detection: validate(some_file.docx) detects docx format → reads word/document.xml. validate(some_file.pptx) detects pptx format → reads ppt/slides/slide1.xml. Unsupported extension (e.g. .xlsx) → InvalidDocumentError caught → XML-001 report.

V-MP-VALIDATE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES — tmp_path for saved docs; read-only shared rules/*.yaml.

V-MP-FIX.source: src/mint_python/fix.py — pure Python successor to M-FIX (src/mint/fix.py). Auto-fix engine: backup → iterate safe/visual fixes → re-validate → cascade detection. Destructive fixes rejected.
V-MP-FIX.note: Scope limitation (known from M-FIX): _apply_simple_fix() only implements fix logic for D-H09 (raw newline replacement). All other safe/visual rules (D-H01, D-H04..D-H08, D-H10, D-S01..D-S05) have no concrete fix implementation in Phase-9 scope. _apply_simple_fix() returns False for non-D-H09 rule_ids — this is the documented contract. Extending fix coverage to other rules is a future phase task.
V-MP-FIX.depends: MP-VALIDATE (run_checks, ValidationReport, InvalidDocumentError, SeverityMode); MP-RULES (FixCategory, Violation, Severity)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: MODIFY-WITHOUT-BACKUP: apply_fixes() modifies document without create_backup() first.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: SILENT-DESTRUCTIVE: DESTRUCTIVE violation processed without raising DestructiveRejectedError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: INFINITE-LOOP: iterates beyond max_iterations without CascadeDetectedError.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: STALE-FIX-SET: re-uses same violation list across iterations without re-validating.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] — fires once per create_backup(); payload: backup_path. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP / # END_BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP.
required-log-markers.marker-2: [MP-Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] — fires once per apply_fixes(); payload: iterations, applied_fixes, diff. Wrapped via # START_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX / # END_BLOCK_APPLY_FIX.

module-checks.check-4: python -c "from mint_python.fix import fix, DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS; assert DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS == 3"

scenarios.scenario-1: Backup created: create_backup(doc) creates .bak with identical content.
scenarios.scenario-2: Safe fix D-H09: raw_newline.docx → FixReport with iterations≥1, applied_fixes containing 'D-H09'. Post-fix re-validation shows 0 D-H09 violations.
scenarios.scenario-3: Destructive rejected: D-H02 (symbol font, destructive) raises DestructiveRejectedError before file modification.
scenarios.scenario-4: Cascade detected: apply_fixes with max_iterations=2 on a violation set where fix A reverses fix B across iterations (file hash oscillates: H-iter-1 ≠ H-iter-2 but H-iter-2 == H-iter-original). CascadeDetectedError raised after iteration_count exceeds max_iterations with no convergence. The detection uses original_hash comparison (M-FIX parity: engine.py:178 — `new_hash == original_hash`). MP-FIX optionally improves this to previous-iteration hash in a follow-up, but the initial ship matches M-FIX behavior.
scenarios.scenario-5: No violations: empty violations → FixReport iterations=1, applied_fixes=[], diff="no changes".
scenarios.scenario-6: Trace: BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP + BLOCK_APPLY_FIX fired with correct payloads.
scenarios.scenario-7: fix() convenience: fix(doc_path) → run_checks → apply_fixes → FixReport.

V-MP-FIX.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE-WITH-ISOLATED-FIXTURES — ALL mutations target tmp_path copies; backup always first; destructive rejected; cascade bounded.

Phase-7 (chart-free) golden doc remains green — non-regression guard. Foundation ready for Phase-9 (MP-RULES + MP-VALIDATE pure-Python successors).

required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-014 trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold on the e2e chart golden document
required-evidence.evidence-3: M-VALIDATE lenient mode passes on the chart golden document with hard_count=0
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-013 (Phase-7 chart-free golden doc) re-runs green — fingerprint matches mp_e2e_baseline.json
required-evidence.evidence-5: Section.add_chart is concrete — V-MP-SECTION post-Phase-8 scenarios verify positive path; legacy "raises NotImplementedError" scenarios retired
required-evidence.evidence-6: MP-SDK exports Chart per handover §3.4 — V-MP-SDK scenario extends to cover

Document.validate(level) and Document.fix(strategy) unstubbed. Golden doc passes lenient MP-VALIDATE with hard_count=0; fix pipeline converges on bad fixtures; destructive violations rejected. VF-013 + VF-014 non-regression green. Coverage 100% on all new mint_python modules.

required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-015 trace-sequence + 6 invariants hold on validate+fix pipeline
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-016: Document.validate/fix return concrete reports; BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD absent from validate/fix paths

required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-014 (Phase-8 chart golden doc) re-runs green — fingerprint matches mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json
